Howdy folks. I thought I'd try to give team building a go. I wanted to use Ryker since he's pretty neat and I want to gamble while I gamble. This is what I've come up with so far

Ancient embers are kind of weird design to me, or more like they're weird in the context of how they're designing other things. Like, it seems like they're pushing the game to have "burst turns", with Divine Aequor having a 3-turn CD, Divine Ultra having a 3-turn CD, literally everything having a 3-turn CD XD. Doesn't seem to mesh well with the idea of "you should be doing a consistent amount of damage every turn over x-turns", unless they're intending for the fights to take 21+ turns on certain bosses assuming you only clear the embers when you burst XD
embers are literally a design for consistent damage and not for burst turns
Yea, that was what I said, that's why they seem strange in the context of how they're designing characters
theyre not tho
your team should be able to deal enough damage to clear embers outside of burst turns or enough damage in burst turns where it doesnt rly matter

I mean, either way one concept doesn't mesh with the other. If you use a third of a combo to try dealing damage every turn you're not going to have it when you flip into Raging or whatever. Playing weak damage like strikes isn't gonna clear embers when the bosses have millions of HP XD
Like yay, I did 63 damage to that 2 million HP boss, I feel so accomplished proccing those 63 embers XD
maybe build a stronger team
there are enough characters and you have enough tools to proc them consistently
Like who? What types of teams do consistent damage outside of anything they need to hold onto for specific turns?
kath, pollux, discard, vortice, poison, counter
chaos 24, (pontos probably)
Holy, Pontos's damage for early game is insane lol. His X card is genuinely oneshotting every single mob i see.
after your first gmurphy burst sacrifice will clear embers too
mouche pursuit will clear it too

And do any of them try to make use of the new 3-turn CD burst realms or other burst mechanics? I mean, the whole thing I was saying was that two different mechs conflict. The chaos ones don't have a realm with a CD, a lot of the other listed characters also don't have divine realms since they're older and part of the older design philosophy, Vortice doesn't even use her exalt unless you have a bunch of copies because it's actively detrimental to the consistent damage part of her gameplan, even Pontos switching into red stance is actively detrimental if you're trying to make use of his passives. A lot of the new characters with the "burst realms" just ignore their altered realm entirely in favor of doing consistent damage instead. TBF I haven't played Pollux so he could be exactly what I'm looking for. Whale gmurphy is a good call too, next time she comes around I can try pulling 6-14 more copies to get that one going
you just misunderstand the mechanic

If you mean I'm misunderstanding the point of the new divine realms then I guess I probably am. The normal realms don't have any design conflicts with the other mechanics. Maybe the issue is that I started wrong since I guess embers probably came before divine realms.  Divine realms would be the one with the weird design choices in the context of other mechs, lol
embers is just reverse cocoon
apparently ppl hated cocoon so bad
so they just made reverse cocoon (theyre functionally equivalent outside of max hp% atks)
i mean thats the whole point of cocoon
to prevent u from 1tapping in 1turn
embers serves the same purpose
aequor base realm has a design conflict with every game mechanic because it gets outscaled very fast, provides bad shielding and doesnt help the awakeners thrive
divine aequor gives you tools to play lategame
i think they just wanna prevent degenerate gamplay like stacking 3 billion RM and white stance to tank every atk every turn
aequor was considered the weakest arc 2 realm before for a reason
